item.title: Across The Board - Board Gaming Club
item.description: Across The Board is a free club to learn, play and bring your own board games! 18+ only

item.title: Pilates Fridays
item.description:
This group is a small clinical Pilates class – perfect for anyone with injuries or back pain as a way to get stronger and back to activities. Offering small group classes ensures that the supervision of the exercises is excellent and the student quickly develops the confidence to build Pilates into their everyday life.

Our Pilates Teachers are also Chartered Physiotherapists, so we fully understands any medical condition you may have and can successfully modify Pilates exercises to suit you. If you have Health Insurance you may be able to claim for Pilates as part of your Rehabilitation.

item.title: Textiles & General Drop-in
item.description: The Makery is a volunteer-run community workshop. We have drop-ins where we can help you on your making and fixing projects.

item.title: Calderdale Adult Learning - ESOL
item.description: The course will equip you with the confidence to use the English language effectively in various contexts.

item.title: Banking Hub
item.description:
Our Banking Hub, provided by Cash Access, operates Monday – Friday. The Banking Hub has a counter where you can access cash services whenever the hub is open and whoever you bank with:

pay in cash (notes and coins) and cheques

withdraw cash (notes and coins)

check your balance

pay utility bills

top up your gas and electricity

access change-giving services (for registered businesses) 

You can also talk to a community banker from your bank on the day your bank is in the hub. There is a private space where you can talk face-to-face about more complicated banking enquiries like payments and transfers, managing your account, and how to use online banking. You can view the services available from community bankers from the Community Banker Services page on the Cash Access website.

Monday – Virgin Money

Tuesday – Lloyds

Wednesday – Halifax

Thursday – Natwest

Friday – Barclays

item.title: Adult Guitar Practice Sessions
item.description:
Come and join The Pennine Guitar Centre, to have fun learning to play the guitar.

Please contact Kevin for more information.

item.title: Judo Club
item.description: The focus of the club is teaching Judo to the younger generations as well as self-defence for adults. All skills levels are welcome.

item.title: Men in Sheds
item.description: Men in Sheds social sessions at Todmorden Makery for makin’, fixin’ and natterin’.

item.title: Art Class
item.description:
Join a creative community, learn new skills and develop your own art style in a supportive environment! These Art classes are for all abilities and include drawing, painting, print, collage, etc. + exhibitions and trips! 19+

To book & enquire please contact Lisa –

item.title: Pilates Thursdays
item.description:
This group is a small clinical Pilates class – perfect for anyone with injuries or back pain as a way to get stronger and back to activities. Offering small group classes ensures that the supervision of the exercises is excellent and the student quickly develops the confidence to build Pilates into their everyday life.

Our Pilates Teachers are also Chartered Physiotherapists, so we fully understands any medical condition you may have and can successfully modify Pilates exercises to suit you. If you have Health Insurance you may be able to claim for Pilates as part of your Rehabilitation.

item.title: Calderdale Adult Learning - Functional Skills (Maths)
item.description: Qualifications in Maths from Entry Level through to Level 2. Develop your Maths skills with tasks adapted for different learning styles and abilities.

item.title: Play Development Service - For ages 5-14
item.description:
Play Development Services provide free open access sessions for children and young people, a place to try out new activities and making new friends whilst having fun!

We provide inclusive a sport and play. We have a range of activities that are chosen by the children and young people.

The activities sometimes get messy, so please come in clothes that are appropriate for the activities and the weather. We aim for all our activities to be inclusive to all.

We operate on a first come, first served basis as places. In our busy periods we advise you to come early to avoid disappointment as places are limited are are filled quickly.

We ask children and young people with SEND to book so that we can meet their particular needs.

item.title: Life Drawing Thursdays
item.description:
Join us every Wednesday for a relaxed and creative life drawing session! Whether you’re a beginner or an experienced artist, this group is open to everyone who wants to practice their drawing skills in a relaxed and supportive environment. Grab your sketchbook and pencils and come unleash your creativity with us!

Sessions are untutored and self directed.

Boards and Easels are provided, materials are not, but you are welcome to bring anything you would like. There are sinks in the room for paint/ink/clay etc.

Please contact Sophie about any access requirements and she will do her best to accommodate you.

item.title: Sing & Breathe
item.description: Singing for anyone affected by a long term lung condition from asthma to COPD. Supported by Asthma + Lung UK

item.title: Banking Hub
item.description:
Our Banking Hub, provided by Cash Access, operates Monday – Friday. The Banking Hub has a counter where you can access cash services whenever the hub is open and whoever you bank with:

pay in cash (notes and coins) and cheques

withdraw cash (notes and coins)

check your balance

pay utility bills

top up your gas and electricity

access change-giving services (for registered businesses) 

You can also talk to a community banker from your bank on the day your bank is in the hub. There is a private space where you can talk face-to-face about more complicated banking enquiries like payments and transfers, managing your account, and how to use online banking. You can view the services available from community bankers from the Community Banker Services page on the Cash Access website.

Monday – Virgin Money

Tuesday – Lloyds

Wednesday – Halifax

Thursday – Natwest

Friday – Barclays

item.title: Iyengar Yoga
item.description:
Join Debbie Bartholomew for Iyengar Yoga classes in Todmorden! Whatever your experience or ability come along to class and learn the art of Iyengar Yoga in a fun and relaxed environment.

Iyengar Yoga is a long established approach which helps students to build confidence, fitness and wellbeing.  In class we focus on alignment, precision, extension and breath which helps improve the sense of ease in mind and body.

Debbie is an extremely knowledgable teacher with over 30 years experience.  She holds the level 3 Iyengar teaching qualification and the worldwide Iyengar Yoga certification mark which assures quality and excellence in teaching.

To book please use the contact infromation below:

item.title: Life Drawing Wednesdays
item.description:
Join us every Wednesday for a relaxed and creative life drawing session! Whether you’re a beginner or an experienced artist, this group is open to everyone who wants to practice their drawing skills in a relaxed and supportive environment. Grab your sketchbook and pencils and come unleash your creativity with us!

Sessions are untutored and self directed.

Boards and Easels are provided, materials are not, but you are welcome to bring anything you would like. There are sinks in the room for paint/ink/clay etc.

Please contact Sophie about any access requirements and she will do her best to accommodate you.

item.title: Todmorden Karate Club
item.description: Traditional Shotokan Karate, Taught by 7th Dan Black Belt , Bob Smith.

item.title: Walking Netball
item.description: Walking netball, a great way to be sociable with fun activities and exercise!

item.title: Electricals & General Drop-in
item.description: Work on or receive help with your electrical projects/items.

item.title: Queer Creatives
item.description:
Do you want to meet others for support and motivation and to ‘Get Stuff Done’!? For those who are queer identifying, we offer a safe place to meet, create, share and chat.

This group is free, but if you are able to make a small donation, it is appreciated.

item.title: Textiles & Quiet Drop-in
item.description: Todmorden Makery is a member-run space where volunteers help people do the following activities in drop-in and workshop sessions.

item.title: Tool Library & Woodworking Drop-in
item.description: Todmorden Makery is a member-run space where volunteers help people do the following activities in drop-in and workshop sessions.

pay in cash (notes and coins) and cheques

withdraw cash (notes and coins)

check your balance

pay utility bills

top up your gas and electricity

You can also talk to a community banker from your bank on the day your bank is in the hub. There is a private space where you can talk face-to-face about more complicated banking enquiries like payments and transfers, managing your account, and how to use online banking. You can view the services available from community bankers from the Community Banker Services page on the Cash Access website.

Monday – Virgin Money

Tuesday – Lloyds

Wednesday – Halifax

Thursday – Natwest

Friday – Barclays

item.title: Women in Workshops
item.description: A workshop space for women to pursue their own woodworking projects or learn how to, in a safe and collaborative environment.

item.title: Rollerskating
item.description: Join Tod Rollers, a friendly and welcoming community roller skating group based in Todmorden College every Tuesday! Bring your own skates. 18+ only. Any questions? Please WhatsApp or Email Gina. See you there!

item.title: Secrets of Christ Church by Sarah Browning
item.description:
Join Sarah to learn about all the graves at Christ Church, Todmorden, that you might have missed while walking through. From plot markers, stones that tell you everything and stones that give away nothing until you start searching. Not all people here went peacefully to their graves, so you will also hear about suicides and murders.

Sarah is a library assistant at Todmorden Library and graveyard enthusiast who thinks that both locations are the perfect place to begin learning about a place’s history.

item.description:

item.title: Table Tennis - Open Session
item.description: Table Tennis every Tuesday for anyone! Bats and balls available.

item.description:
This group is a small clinical Pilates class – perfect for anyone with injuries or back pain as a way to get stronger and back to activities. Offering small group classes ensures that the supervision of the exercises is excellent and the student quickly develops the confidence to build Pilates into their everyday life.

Our Pilates Teachers are also Chartered Physiotherapists, so we fully understands any medical condition you may have and can successfully modify Pilates exercises to suit you. If you have Health Insurance you may be able to claim for Pilates as part of your Rehabilitation.

item.description: Qualifications in Maths from Entry Level through to Level 2. Develop your Maths skills with tasks adapted for different learning styles and abilities.

item.description:
Join us every Wednesday for a relaxed and creative life drawing session! Whether you’re a beginner or an experienced artist, this group is open to everyone who wants to practice their drawing skills in a relaxed and supportive environment. Grab your sketchbook and pencils and come unleash your creativity with us!

Sessions are untutored and self directed.

Boards and Easels are provided, materials are not, but you are welcome to bring anything you would like. There are sinks in the room for paint/ink/clay etc.

Please contact Sophie about any access requirements and she will do her best to accommodate you.

item.title: Sing & Breathe
item.description: Singing for anyone affected by a long term lung condition from asthma to COPD. Supported by Asthma + Lung UK

item.title: First Aid at Work Blended or Requal (3 day or 2 day)
item.description:
The course is suitable for people who work in high risk workplaces and covers the worst case scenarios and some of the more common minor stuff and goes on to cover common injuries and conditions a first aider might come across.

3 Days/Blended – 1 day online learning, 2 days in the Splat classroom.

The 3 day /Blended course means that 6 hours of work must be completed online prior to starting of the 2 day course. Without this completed online element, delegates will not be able to attend training and places cannot be refunded. (Registration for the online element will be sent separately when booking is complete)

2 Days/Requalifier – 2 days in the Splat classroom.

You are eligible for the 2-day requalifier, if you have a current ‘First Aid at Work’ certificate.

First Aid Training at Splat is relaxed and enjoyable. We aim to help you learn and support you to get what you need from the day. There are some practical and some multiple-choice assessments but our job is to ensure you have everything you need to pass these easily.

Please note that First Aid can be quite physical, you will need to be able to kneel on the floor to perform CPR and Recovery Position. Please wear comfortable clothing you can move around in, and, if possible, please let us know ahead of time if there is anything we can do to help support you and your enjoyment of the day.

item.title: Banking Hub
item.description:
Our Banking Hub, provided by Cash Access, operates Monday – Friday. The Banking Hub has a counter where you can access cash services whenever the hub is open and whoever you bank with:

pay in cash (notes and coins) and cheques

withdraw cash (notes and coins)

check your balance

pay utility bills

top up your gas and electricity

access change-giving services (for registered businesses) 

You can also talk to a community banker from your bank on the day your bank is in the hub. There is a private space where you can talk face-to-face about more complicated banking enquiries like payments and transfers, managing your account, and how to use online banking. You can view the services available from community bankers from the Community Banker Services page on the Cash Access website.

Monday – Virgin Money

Tuesday – Lloyds

Wednesday – Halifax

Thursday – Natwest

Friday – Barclays

item.title: Art Therapy
item.description: A safe, gentle healing creative group for people who have experienced life challenges, such as (but not exclusively) trauma, anxiety or depression.

item.title: Pickleball
item.description: Weekly sessions of Pickleball. Please contact Angela before attending. (This class is currently full, if any spots become available, please contact Angela and she will let you know).

item.title: Andys Man Club
item.description:
Andys Man Club offer a free to attend peer-to-peer support group every Monday, except bank holidays. In these groups, men can open up about the storms affecting their lives in a safe, judgement-free and non-clinical environment. These clubs are designed to be free of pressure, there is no obligation for men to speak, they can simply listen if they wish. We know it can be daunting contemplating coming to a club with people you don’t know, but don’t be afraid to come along and see for yourself. It might be the best decision you make. Please remember this group is 18+

ANDYSMANCLUB are a men’s suicide prevention charity that aims to end the stigma surrounding men’s mental health and help men through the power of conversation. #ITSOKAYTOTALK

item.title: Andys Man Club
item.description:
Andys Man Club offer a free to attend peer-to-peer support group every Monday, except bank holidays. In these groups, men can open up about the storms affecting their lives in a safe, judgement-free and non-clinical environment. These clubs are designed to be free of pressure, there is no obligation for men to speak, they can simply listen if they wish. We know it can be daunting contemplating coming to a club with people you don’t know, but don’t be afraid to come along and see for yourself. It might be the best decision you make. Please remember this group is 18+

ANDYSMANCLUB are a men’s suicide prevention charity that aims to end the stigma surrounding men’s mental health and help men through the power of conversation. #ITSOKAYTOTALK

item.title: Todmorden Karate Club
item.description: Traditional Shotokan Karate, Taught by 7th Dan Black Belt , Bob Smith.

item.title: Kixx Football - For children aged 3-8
item.description:
Kixx is a children’s football academy, who provide a high-energy, fun, and imaginative introduction to physical activity, all through the power of football.

Kixx sessions are also a brilliant way for children to build friendships and grow in confidence. Every class is carefully designed for both boys and girls aged 18 months to 10 years, ensuring the activities are age-appropriate, inclusive, and engaging.

The Kixx curriculum focuses on developing both physical skills and social skills — helping every child learn, move, and thrive in a supportive environment.

Our vision is simple: to inspire every child to be better through football.

Or you can directly contact Coach Cam – camerontimberley@kixx.org.uk

pay in cash (notes and coins) and cheques

You can also talk to a community banker from your bank on the day your bank is in the hub. item.title: Test Title 4
item.description: TESt/

item.title: Test 2 b
item.description: test

item.title: Test 2
item.description: test

item.title: Family Coding Club
item.description:
An informal drop-in coding session for children and families. Activities include simple games, creative coding, and playful experiments with computers.

No prior experience needed. Suitable for ages 7+ with an accompanying adult.

item.title: Game jam taster, build a tiny game in two hours
item.description:
A relaxed introduction to game jams. We will form small teams (or work solo), pick a playful theme, and build a tiny game using a starter template.

No experience needed. Bring a laptop if you can.

item.title: Monday Website Club - Feb
item.description: A test event

item.title: test1
item.description: test 3

item.title: Repair Café
item.description:
A regular Repair Café where volunteers help fix household items such as small appliances, clothing, and bikes. Bring along something broken and see if it can be repaired rather than thrown away.

No charge, donations welcome.

item.title: Pixel art for games, characters and tiles
item.description:
A practical session focused on making simple sprites, character animations, and tiles for top-down and platform games.

Beginners welcome. Suitable for ages 9+ with an accompanying adult.

item.title: Side missions and mini quests workshop
item.description:
A short, guided workshop on designing side missions and mini quests to add choice, challenge, and humour to your game.

Bring a laptop if you can. We will have a small number available to borrow.

item.title: JAMM Labs game making drop-in
item.description:
An informal drop-in for children, young people, and families to explore game making using simple templates, pixel art, and playful experiments.

Suitable for ages 8+ with an accompanying adult. No prior coding experience needed.

item.title: JAMM Labs game making drop-in
item.description:
An informal drop-in for children, young people, and families to explore game making using simple templates, pixel art, and playful experiments.

Suitable for ages 8+ with an accompanying adult. No prior coding experience needed.

item.description:
An informal drop-in for children, young people, and families to explore game making using simple templates, pixel art, and playful experiments.

Suitable for ages 8+ with an accompanying adult. No prior coding experience needed.

item.description:
An informal drop-in for children, young people, and families to explore game making using simple templates, pixel art, and playful experiments.
